verbGlossary.ACCEPTANCE TESTED FOR: {source} must complete a full-load acceptance test witnessed by {target} to confirm design flow and pressure.
verbGlossary.APRON CUT APPROVED BY: Cutting into the public right-of-way for the paving element's driveway apron requires approval from {target} authority.
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZED BY: {source} work scope is legally permitted to proceed only after {target} is granted (e.g., excavation authorized by building permit).
verbGlossary.BACKFLOW TESTED UNDER: {source}'s backflow prevention device must pass a certification test required by {target} permit authority.
verbGlossary.BALANCED PRIOR TO: {source} element must be tested, adjusted, and balanced before {target} can be issued, confirming design airflow and temperatures.
verbGlossary.CAPACITY SIZED PER: {source}'s sizing and capacity calculations must conform to requirements established in {target} (e.g., storm drainage sized per stormwater permit).
verbGlossary.COMMISSIONING CERTIFIED BY: {source} must complete a functional performance test certified by {target}.
verbGlossary.CONCEALS BEFORE: {source} covers or buries prior work that must receive {target} sign-off before it can be hidden (e.g., backfill conceals utilities before AHJ inspection).
verbGlossary.COVERAGE MAPPED FOR: {source} head layout and coverage must be verified against NFPA spacing requirements by {target}.
verbGlossary.DIMENSIONS COMPLIANT WITH: {source}'s size, height, and placement must conform to dimensional limits established by {target} approval.
verbGlossary.DISCHARGE GOVERNED BY: {source}produced by {target}'s installation process falls under the target permit's effluent or discharge limits.
verbGlossary.EGRESS REVIEWED BY: {source}'s dimensions, rise/run, handrail details, and egress capacity are checked against building code by {target}.
verbGlossary.ENVELOPE REVIEWED BY: {source} thermal, moisture, and structural performance is evaluated by {target}.
verbGlossary.FIXTURE COUNT APPROVED BY: {source}of plumbing fixtures must meet minimum code ratios reviewed by {target}.
verbGlossary.FLOOD TESTED BY: {source} undergoes a standing water or electronic leak detection test witnessed by the target third-party tester.
verbGlossary.FLOW TESTED FOR: {source} must demonstrate adequate flow capacity through a pressure or flow test required by {target}.
verbGlossary.FOOTING INSPECTED BY: {source}'s excavation, reinforcement, and formwork must pass {target} before concrete placement.
verbGlossary.HEIGHT LIMITED BY: {source}'s maximum allowable height is restricted by dimensional standards in {target} approval.
verbGlossary.HUNG AFTER SIGN-OFF FROM: {source} installation may proceed only after {target} authority signs off on all concealed rough-in work behind the wall plane.
verbGlossary.HYDRO TESTED FOR: {source} main must pass a hydrostatic pressure test witnessed by {target} is charged.
verbGlossary.ILLUMINATION REQUIRED FOR: {source}levels in {target}'s coverage area must be demonstrated before the target occupancy certificate is granted.
verbGlossary.IMPACT RATED BY: {source} must demonstrate impact resistance through testing performed or accepted by the target third-party testing lab (e.g., hurricane-zone window testing).
verbGlossary.INSTALLED PRIOR TO: {source} must be fully installed and established before {target} of completion can be issued.
verbGlossary.INSTALLED UNDER: {source} is installed pursuant to and in compliance with {target}-specific permit (e.g., ductwork installed under mechanical permit).
verbGlossary.LEAK TESTED UNDER: {source}'s piping joints must pass a leak test at prescribed pressure, as required by {target} permit.
verbGlossary.LIFT COORDINATED WITH: {source}s requires scheduling coordination with {target} authority to allow for in-progress observation.
verbGlossary.LOAD TESTED BY: {source} must pass a full-load and safety test administered by {target} authority before public use.
verbGlossary.MONITORING ACTIVE BEFORE: {source} must be connected to a monitoring service and confirmed operational before {target} is issued.
verbGlossary.MORTAR SAMPLED BY: {source} mortar and grout specimens are collected and tested by {target} compressive strength.
verbGlossary.OBSERVED DURING PLACEMENT BY: {source}inspector must witness {target}'s installation in real time as required by code (e.g., deep foundation pile driving observed by special inspection).
verbGlossary.PANEL SCHEDULE FILED WITH: {source}'s circuit directory and load calculations are submitted to and reviewed by {target} permit authority.
verbGlossary.PHOTOMETRIC VERIFIED FOR: {source}'s light output and distribution pattern must meet minimum site lighting levels required for {target}.
verbGlossary.POUR WITNESSED BY: {source} placement for the structural element must be directly observed by the target special inspector to verify reinforcement, mix, and procedures.
verbGlossary.PRESSURE TESTED UNDER: {source} must pass a pressure test at a threshold specified by {target} permit before it can be put into service.
verbGlossary.R-VALUE CONFIRMED BY: {source}'s installed thermal resistance value is checked against energy code minimums by {target} authority.
verbGlossary.RATED ASSEMBLY APPROVED BY: {source}'s fire-resistance rating and listed assembly details must be reviewed and accepted by {target}.
verbGlossary.REFRIGERANT REGISTERED UNDER: {source} refrigerant type and charge quantity must be registered with {target} authority per EPA regulations.
verbGlossary.RISER TESTED FOR: {source} riser must pass a hydrostatic pressure and flow test witnessed by {target} to confirm code-compliant water delivery to each floor.
verbGlossary.ROUGH-IN INSPECTED BY: {source}'s concealed installation is inspected by {target} before it is covered by finishes.
verbGlossary.SATISFIES: {source} fulfills a specific condition or control measure required by {target} (e.g., erosion control satisfies stormwater permit BMP requirements).
verbGlossary.SEQUENCE VERIFIED BY: {source}'s control sequences and programming are verified against design intent by the target third-party commissioning agent.
verbGlossary.SERVICE RELEASED BY: {source}provider, coordinated through the target permit process, must release or energize {target}'s service connection.
verbGlossary.SMOKE RATING LABELED FOR: {source} smoke and fire rating label must be verified as code-compliant by {target}.
verbGlossary.STACK TESTED FOR: {source}'s drain, waste, and vent piping must pass a water or air pressure test administered by {target} authority.
verbGlossary.STARTUP SCHEDULED WITH: {source}'s operational startup and test run must be scheduled with {target} authority so they can witness performance.
verbGlossary.STRUCTURAL REVIEWED BY: {source}'s engineering design and calculations are reviewed by {target} compliance before construction.
verbGlossary.TAP APPROVED BY: Connection of {target} to a public utility main requires approval from the target permit authority before the physical tap is made.
verbGlossary.TRANSFER TESTED FOR: {source} automatic transfer switch must demonstrate successful changeover in a test witnessed by {target}.
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: Installing or beginning work on {target} initiates a required regulatory filing or notification (e.g., deep excavation triggers OSHA notification).
verbGlossary.WELD CERTIFIED BY: Structural welds on {target} must be inspected and certified by the target special inspection authority, often via ultrasonic or radiographic testing.
